name: Maestro
description: Six-channel clocked modulation controller · DAW-automation feel in eurorack · 8 waveforms per channel with smooth, bipolar, triplet, slow modifiers · 14 save slots on microSD · internal or external clock · chain mode sequences waveforms per channel.

controls.name: Channel buttons I–VI
controls.description: Select one or more channels for subsequent edits. Hold during save/load to restrict data to those channels.

controls.name: Waveform buttons
controls.description: Eight waveforms: rising saw, falling saw, triangle up, triangle down, square, random plus high/low variants in the clock-menu overlay.
controls.detail: saw-up · saw-down · ramp-up · ramp-down · square · random · (high, low clock overlays)

controls.name: smooth
controls.description: Applies slew to the selected waveform, converting stepped ramps into curved shapes and square into pulse-wave.
controls.detail: toggle · combines with any waveform · random+smooth becomes smooth random/S&H-like curve

controls.name: bipolar
controls.description: Switches the channel between 0..+5V (unipolar) and −5..+5V (bipolar) output range.
controls.detail: toggle · unipolar default · bipolar doubles available span

controls.name: Timing buttons 1/2, 1/4, 1/8, 1/16, 1/32
controls.description: Clock division per channel, measured in 4 pulses per quarter note.
controls.detail: from 1/2 (slow) to 1/32 (fast) · combines with triplet and slow for extended timings

controls.name: triplet
controls.description: Turns the selected timing into its triplet variant (e.g. 1/4 → 1/4t).

controls.name: slow
controls.description: Extends the selected timing to multi-bar ranges, up to 32 bars / 128 clock pulses.

controls.name: mute
controls.description: Mutes or unmutes the channel's output. In clock menu mode, starts/stops the internal clock.
controls.detail: toggle · channel LED turns off when muted

controls.name: chain
controls.description: Enters chain mode to sequence multiple waveforms on one channel. Each waveform can keep the channel timing or take a per-step timing.
controls.detail: enter → pick waveforms in order → select channels → press chain to apply

controls.name: clock
controls.description: Opens clock-settings menu where waveform buttons act as tap tempo, ±5 bpm, random tempo, reset-to-120bpm and start/stop.
controls.detail: high: manual reset · low: 120bpm reset · ramp-up: tap · triangle-up: +5bpm · triangle-down: −5bpm · random: 60–180bpm · slow+knob: 1bpm steps

controls.name: save
controls.description: Opens save menu. Middle two rows (14 buttons) map to 14 save slots on the microSD card.
controls.detail: channel-selected → per-channel save · no selection → whole-module state

controls.name: load
controls.description: Opens load menu. Loads instantly from internal memory (preloaded from SD at boot) so performance recalls are gap-free.
controls.detail: 14 slots · channel-selected → partial load · no selection → full state

inputs.name: clock in
inputs.description: External clock input. When a cable is inserted, Maestro locks to the incoming edges at 4 PPQN and the clock menu tempo controls are disabled.

inputs.name: reset in
inputs.description: Resets all six channels' phase to the start of their current waveform or chain step.

inputs.name: gate / trig in I–VI
inputs.description: Per-channel gate or trigger input. Behaviour depends on channel configuration — can re-trigger the waveform or advance chain steps.

outputs.name: clock out
outputs.description: Internal clock distributed at 4 PPQN. Active whether Maestro is internally or externally clocked.

outputs.name: reset out
outputs.description: Reset pulse, firing at the start of each bar or when a manual reset is triggered.

outputs.name: channel out I–VI
outputs.description: CV output for each of the 6 channels. Range follows bipolar toggle: 0 to +5V or −5 to +5V.

behaviors.name: Chain playback per channel
behaviors.trigger: chain menu → select waveforms in order → apply to channels
behaviors.description: A channel plays a chained sequence of waveforms, each lasting one cycle of its timing. Optional per-step timings override the channel's default. Produces modulation phrases that evolve over multiple bars without patching extra modules.

behaviors.name: Per-channel clock division
behaviors.trigger: selecting channels and pressing a timing button
behaviors.description: Every channel runs at its own division, always phase-locked to the master clock. Mix 1/16 detail on one lane with 1/4s (slow) on another for layered timescales from the same clock.

behaviors.name: Random + smooth = smooth random
behaviors.description: Stepped random values get interpolated between change points, turning the channel into a clock-synced smooth-random source similar to a sampled-and-held noise into a slew limiter — but always locked to the grid.

behaviors.name: Bipolar doubles voltage span
behaviors.description: Switches output from 0..+5V to −5..+5V. Useful for FM, PWM, and attenuverter-free destinations that expect centred modulation. Waveform shape is unchanged; only the span flips.

behaviors.name: Gate-triggered one-shot
behaviors.trigger: cable in a channel's gate/trig input
behaviors.description: Once patched, the channel can run as a gate-triggered one-shot envelope rather than free-running, re-triggering on each incoming pulse — effectively converting that lane into a clocked AR/AD envelope.

behaviors.name: Instant-recall save slots
behaviors.description: All 14 slots are preloaded from the microSD to RAM at power-up, so pressing load jumps to a new state instantly with no SD read gap. Full-module or per-channel scope depending on whether any channel is selected.

behaviors.name: Internal clock tap & random tempo
behaviors.trigger: clock menu entered and clock input unpatched
behaviors.description: In the clock menu, the ramp-up waveform button taps tempo, up/down adjust ±5 bpm (±1 with slow held), and random sets the internal clock to a value between 60 and 180 bpm. Pressing mute stops and restarts the clock.

behaviors.name: Triplet × slow extended timings
behaviors.trigger: triplet and/or slow combined with a timing button
behaviors.description: Opens the range from 1/32 all the way to 32 bars (128 pulses at 4 PPQN). Triplet-slow values fill in between the straight divisions for polymetric phrasing from the same grid.

patchIdeas.name: Six-lane synced modulation bus
patchIdeas.patch: Master clock → clock in · CH I: 1/16 ramp-up for hat accent · CH II: 1/4 square bipolar → VCA punch · CH III: 1/2 triangle → filter cutoff · CH IV: 1/4s random+smooth → resonance · CH V: 1/8 square for arpeggio decay · CH VI: 1 bar slow ramp → pan sweep · one module replaces six LFOs
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* CH I: 1/16 ramp-up
* CH II: 1/4 square · bipolar
* CH III: 1/2 triangle
* CH IV: 1/4s random + smooth
* CH V: 1/8 square
* CH VI: 1 bar slow ramp

Clock Source:
Hat VCA:
Kick VCA:
Filter:
VCA:
Arp Env:
Pan:

- Clock Source (Out) c> Maestro (clock in)
- Maestro (channel out I) >> Hat VCA (CV)
- Maestro (channel out II) >> Kick VCA (CV)
- Maestro (channel out III) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Maestro (channel out IV) >> Filter (Resonance)
- Maestro (channel out V) >> Arp Env (Level)
- Maestro (channel out VI) >> Pan (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Chained waveform modulation phrase
patchIdeas.patch: Enter chain menu · pick ramp-up → triangle → square → random for CH III · assigns one bar each at 1/2 timing · apply to channel · filter cutoff evolves through four distinct shapes every four bars · no external sequencer driving modulation shape
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* CH III: chain = ramp-up, triangle, square, random
* CH III: 1/2 timing (4 bars per cycle)

Clock:
Filter:

- Clock (Out) c> Maestro (clock in)
- Maestro (channel out III) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Gate-triggered AD envelopes
patchIdeas.patch: Drum clock → gate/trig in I and II · CH I: falling-saw + smooth = AR shape · CH II: triangle-down + smooth + bipolar = punchy AD · CH III free-running 1/16 for hats · use Maestro channels as clocked one-shot envelopes
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* CH I: falling saw + smooth
* CH II: triangle-down + smooth + bipolar
* CH III: 1/16 square (free run)

Kick Trig:
Snare Trig:
Clock:
Kick VCA:
Snare VCA:
Hat VCA:

- Clock (Out) c> Maestro (clock in)
- Kick Trig (Out) t> Maestro (gate / trig in I)
- Snare Trig (Out) t> Maestro (gate / trig in II)
- Maestro (channel out I) >> Kick VCA (CV)
- Maestro (channel out II) >> Snare VCA (CV)
- Maestro (channel out III) >> Hat VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Performance recall scene change
patchIdeas.patch: Slot 1 = verse mod set · slot 2 = chorus · slot 3 = bridge · press load + channel buttons II, IV to only swap those two lanes between scenes · bass modulation on CH I and drum mods on CH III, V, VI stay untouched · scene changes with no clock glitch
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* slot 1: verse · slot 2: chorus · slot 3: bridge
* load+CH II, IV → partial load

Clock:
Bass Filter:
Pad Filter:
FX Send:

- Clock (Out) c> Maestro (clock in)
- Maestro (channel out I) >> Bass Filter (Cutoff)
- Maestro (channel out II) >> Pad Filter (Cutoff)
- Maestro (channel out IV) >> FX Send (Level)

patchIdeas.name: Smooth random cutoff + stepped random resonance
patchIdeas.patch: CH I: random + smooth at 1/4 → filter cutoff (smooth sampled-and-held curve) · CH II: random no smooth at 1/8 bipolar → resonance (stepped) · two facets of the same random character on one filter, both clock-locked
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* CH I: random + smooth · 1/4
* CH II: random · 1/8 · bipolar

Clock:
Filter:

- Clock (Out) c> Maestro (clock in)
- Maestro (channel out I) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Maestro (channel out II) >> Filter (Resonance)

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythmic envelope gates
patchIdeas.patch: CH I: 1/4 square · CH II: 1/4t square · CH III: 1/8s square · each bipolar square becomes a gate source when sent to a comparator or envelope trig input · Maestro hosts a six-lane polyrhythmic gate generator using only square outs
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* CH I: square · 1/4
* CH II: square · 1/4t
* CH III: square · 1/8s

Clock:
Envelope A:
Envelope B:
Envelope C:

- Clock (Out) c> Maestro (clock in)
- Maestro (channel out I) g> Envelope A (Trig)
- Maestro (channel out II) g> Envelope B (Trig)
- Maestro (channel out III) g> Envelope C (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Long-form 32-bar evolution
patchIdeas.patch: CH VI: ramp-up + slow on 1/32 = a 32-bar rising ramp · bipolar → feeds filter cutoff across a whole song section · other channels run faster modulations on top · Maestro handles macro-scale evolution no dedicated slow-LFO needed
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* CH VI: ramp-up + slow + bipolar · 1/32s (32 bars)

Clock:
Macro Filter:

- Clock (Out) c> Maestro (clock in)
- Maestro (channel out VI) >> Macro Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Tap-tempo standalone clock hub
patchIdeas.patch: No external clock · enter clock menu · tap on ramp-up button for live BPM · use clock out to master the system · channels I-VI drive sequencer clocks, LFO triggers, reset pulses · Maestro acts as 20HP clock hub + six modulators
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* clock menu: tap tempo live
* CH I: 1/16 square (master clock copy)
* CH II: 1/4 ramp-down

Sequencer:
LFO:
Resetter:

- Maestro (clock out) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- Maestro (reset out) t> Sequencer (Reset)
- Maestro (channel out I) c> LFO (Trig)
- Maestro (channel out II) >> Resetter (In)

patchIdeas.name: Triplet-vs-straight rhythmic layering
patchIdeas.patch: CH I: 1/8 square for straight 8ths · CH II: 1/8t square for triplet 8ths · CH III: 1/16 square for double-time · mix into three separate drum triggers · gives polymetric feel from a single clock without a Euclidean sequencer
patchIdeas.diagram:
Maestro:
* CH I: square · 1/8
* CH II: square · 1/8t
* CH III: square · 1/16

Clock:
Kick:
Hat:
Snare:

- Clock (Out) c> Maestro (clock in)
- Maestro (channel out I) t> Kick (Trig)
- Maestro (channel out II) t> Hat (Trig)
- Maestro (channel out III) t> Snare (Trig)
